notes.note: Ptolemy XII Neos Dionysos, called Auletes (the Flute-Player), king of Egypt; expelled by an Alexandrian uprising in 58 BC, after long lobbying restored to his throne by A. Gabinius's army from Syria in spring 55 BC.
Faustus Cornelius Sulla, son of the dictator (born 86 BC, husband of Pompey's daughter); his Cumae villa held a notable library, perhaps drawing on his father's collection.
Sella curulis: the ivory-inlaid magistrate's seat, the visible insignia of higher imperium. ``Those gentlemen'' are the consuls --- Pompey and Crassus.
Laconicum: a sweat-room in Spartan style, the dry-heat chamber of a Roman bath suite. The Cyrean works are after Cyrus, the architect Atticus and Cicero kept on the Palatine project.
21 April, the festival of Pales, the pastoral goddess; the day Romans celebrated the founding of the city.
